predictions.description: One breadcrumb per If in bot order pins the whole truth table: numeric greaterThan ('10'>'9' numerically, lexically false); loose equals is numeric ('0.30'=='0.3'), case-insensitive ('CSV'=='csv'), and date-instant ('2/12/2026'=='2026-02-12'); equalsExact is literal ('CSV'!=='csv' → else); isEmpty/hasValue on empty string; multi-row and/or evaluates LEFT-TO-RIGHT with no precedence ((a=a AND b=c) OR x=x → true); contains on a list variable is item-wise case-insensitive membership; nested if runs outer-then then inner-else; isNumber('12.5'); ordering comparators on non-numeric non-date strings are FALSE (no lexical ordering) → else branch. Then the empty-vs-blank split, on a whitespace-only value '   ': isBlank TRUE (trim-aware) but isEmpty FALSE (→ else) and hasValue TRUE — so isBlank is the only comparator that catches it — and isNotBlank FALSE (→ else); '' is blank as well; '0' is NOT blank (isNotBlank TRUE — a falsy-looking value still counts as a value). isBlank/isNotBlank were declared in the Comparator union but never implemented, so before this they silently evaluated FALSE and every one of these Ifs took the else branch.
